{Describe the design and implementation of the product. Use UML diagrams and short code snippets where applicable.}
FinTrack Pro was created for individual students in a relationship who are planning to set aside finances for their share of a BTO downpayment.

An individual BTO budget planner for university students planning to apply for BTO that turns messy finances into a clear downpayment plan - showing how much the individual needs to save and whether additional financing is required.
|Version| As a ... | I want to ... | So that I can ...|
|v1.0|new user|see usage instructions|refer to them when I forget how to use the application|
|v2.0|user|find a to-do item by name|locate a to-do without having to go through the entire list|

# FinTrack Pro: From Campus to Keys
FinTrack Pro is for individual students in a relationship who are
planning to set aside finances for their share of a BTO downpayment.
* To know ALL commands needed to run our program, click on our [User Guide](UserGuide.md)
* To understand the structure and inspiration behind our project, click on [Developer Guide](DeveloperGuide.md)
* To know the people behind this cool planner, click on [About Us](AboutUs.md)
FinTrack Pro was created for individual students in a relationship who are planning to set aside finances for their share of a BTO downpayment.
1. Ensure that you have Java 17 or above installed.
2. Down the latest ```.jar``` file of FinTrackPro from [here](https://github.com/AY2526S2-CS2113-T14-2/tP/releases).
3. Copy the file to the folder that you want to use as the home folder for your Task Manager.
4. Open Terminal(Mac) or Windows Powershell(Windows), ```cd``` into the folder you put the jar file in, and use the ```java -jar FinTrackPro.jar``` command to run the application.
5. You should see something an introduction page asking for your name.
6. Type the command in the command line and press Enter to execute it.
7. You can refer to the [Features](#features-) page for details of each command. Have fun!!
Shows a message explaining how to access the help page, lists all commands.
This is your help page. Here are the commands:
Sets or updates your current monthly income to help calculate spending proportions.
You have set your salary as $1200.50/month.
Setting your current liquid assets dedicated to the BTO downpayment.
NOTE: ```AMOUNT``` represents the total cash/CPF you currently have available for the goal.
You have set your current savings as $5000.00/month.
Defines the target BTO price goal
NOTE: ```TOTAL_PRICE``` is the FULL cost of the BTO flat. FinTrackPro will
automatically calculate the 2.5% downpayment requirement plus estimated legal fees.
Your goal is to save $20000.00 (Downpayment plus legal fees)
Defines the proportion of how much you are paying for the downpayment.
You have set the ratio to be 60%. You need to fork out $2,500.00 at the end of your goal of December 2027!
Adds a regular expense to your monthly tracker.
Here is the list of your expenditures!
Shows a list of current month expenses and income entries.
Here is your current expenditure list!
Money Left for the Month based on goal: $2,500.00 / $5,000.00
Deletes the specified entry from the tracker.
You currently have 2 expenses in your expenditure list!
Sets a specific date by which you want to reach your downpayment goal.
You have set December 2027 as your goal deadline! You currently have x years, and y months left to your deadline.
Displays a comprehensive overview of your financial status, including distance to goal.
Deletes all current data and resets the profile
Are you sure you want to clear all current data? (Y/N):
(if Y) You have cleared all current data!
**Q**: How do I transfer my data to another computer?
**A**: Your data will be saved on your local computer. Just log in with the same name
on another device if you want to continue with your saved progress!
Watch this space for more updates!!
| Set Monthly Salary     | `salary amt/AMOUNT` e.g. `salary amt/1200.50`            |
| Set Current Savings    | `savings amt/AMOUNT` e.g. `savings amt/5000`             |
| Set BTO Goal Price     | `goal TOTAL_PRICE` e.g. `goal 400000`                    |
| Add Expense            | `add n/NAME amt/AMOUNT` e.g. `add n/Hall Rental amt/450` |
We hope that you found FinTrackPro useful and easy to use!
Meanwhile, if you have any enquires/bugs that you might have found please email us [here!](mailto:e1406324@u.nus.edu)
public CommandHandler(Ui ui, Profile profile, ExpenseList expenseList, Storage storage) {
* Adds an expense entry into the {@link ExpenseList}.

*
  • Rejects values with more than 2 decimal places

*

On success, prints the new expense and the updated running total.
* @param userInput Full command line entered by the user (starting with {@code add}).
// If there is no input after add
ui.printLine("Format: add <value(to 2dp)> bro! where is the MONEHHHH");
ui.printLine("Amount must be a valid number bro! What is this garbage!");
ui.printLine("Amount cannot be negative bro who you trying to scam?");
ui.printLine("Amount must not exceed 2 decimal places bro we dont want your measly cents!");
* Deletes an expense entry from the {@link ExpenseList} by 1-based index.

*

On success, prints the removed entry and the updated running total.
* @param userInput Full command line entered by the user (starting with {@code delete}).
// If there is no input after delete
ui.printLine("Format: delete <index> bro! where is the INDEXXX");
ui.printLine("Invalid index bro! do you even know how much you've spent?");
* Clears all expenses from the {@link ExpenseList} after user confirmation.

*

This method prompts the user with a confirmation question.
* Only a response of {@code "y"} (case-insensitive after trimming) will proceed.

*

Side effect: Mutates the {@link ExpenseList} by removing all entries if confirmed.
* @param in Scanner used to read the user's confirmation response.
ui.printLine("WARNING: This will permanently delete ALL expenses. Are you sure? (Input Y to clear)");
ui.printLine("Expense list has been wiped clean. Fresh start!");
ui.printLine("Clear cancelled. Your data is still there, bro.");
* Adds a specified amount to the user's current total savings.
* @param in Scanner used to read the user's deposit input.
// Prompt for the amount to add
BigDecimal depositAmount = InputUtil.readMoney(ui, in, "Enter amount to add to your savings:");
// Update profile by adding to the current balance
* Computes and displays a BTO Readiness Report based on the user's current financial profile.

*

Calculates the following metrics from {@link Profile} and {@link ExpenseList}:
*   <li>Estimated months to reach the goal, or a status message if already reached

*

Results are passed to {@link Ui#showSummaryReport} for display.
* Completely resets the user profile and expense list after confirmation.
* @param in Scanner used for user confirmation.
ui.printLine("WARNING: This will wipe your profile and ALL expenses. Type 'Y' to continue: ");
// 2. Overwrite the save file with the empty data
ui.printLine("System reset successful. Please restart or type 'bye' to exit.");
ui.printLine("Error: Could not reset the save file on disk.");
ui.printLine("Reset aborted. Your data is safe!");

* Prints the current expense list and total expenditure.

* Parses raw user input into command tokens.

*

This class is responsible only for extracting the command keyword
* from a full user input line. It does not validate arguments or execute
* Extracts the command keyword from a raw input string.

*

The input is split at the first space character.

*

No validation is performed. The returned string may be empty
* if the input string is empty.
* @return The command keyword (first token before the first space).
* Represents an individual financial expense within the FinTrackPro system.
* Constructs a new {@code Expense} with the specified amount.
* The default category is set to "OTHER".
* @param amount The monetary value of the expense.
* Gets the monetary amount of this expense.
* @return The expense amount as a {@code BigDecimal}.
* Returns a string representation of the expense amount, prefixed with a dollar sign.
* @return a {@code String} in the format {@code "$<amount>"}, e.g. {@code "$42.50"}
* Manages a collection of {@link Expense} objects and tracks the cumulative total.

*

This class provides methods to add, delete, and retrieve expenses while
* automatically updating the running total using {@code BigDecimal} for precision.
private final ArrayList<Expense> expenses = new ArrayList<>();

* and specific targets for BTO downpayment planning.

This date is used to calculate the remaining time in the BTO Readiness Report.

* Handles the persistence of user profile data and expense lists to a local file.
* This class ensures that the financial profile and all tracked expenses are saved
* to a text file on disk and loaded back into the application at startup.
* Initializes a new Storage object with the specified file path.
* @param filePath The path to the text file used for data persistence.
* Saves the current financial state to a text file.
* Format for Profile: {@code P | name | salary | savings | btoGoal | ratio}
* Format for Expenses: {@code E | amount | category}
* @param profile The {@link Profile} containing the user's personal financial goals.
* @param expenseList The {@link ExpenseList} containing all recorded transactions.
* @throws IOException If there is an error writing to the file.
public void save(Profile profile, ExpenseList expenseList) throws IOException {
* Loads financial data from the save file into the provided profile and expense list.
* If the save file does not exist, the method returns without modifying the data.
* It parses each line based on the prefix ("P" for Profile, "E" for Expense) and
* maps the data to the respective object fields.
* @param profile The {@link Profile} object to be populated with saved data.
* @param expenseList The {@link ExpenseList} object to be populated with saved expenses.
* @throws IOException If there is an error reading from the file.
public void load(Profile profile, ExpenseList expenseList) throws IOException {
return; // No save file yet, start with fresh data
* Represents a snapshot of the user's BTO savings readiness.

*

Computed from a {@link Profile} and {@link ExpenseList} at the time of construction.
* All fields are immutable after instantiation.
* Constructs a {@code SummaryReport} from the user's current profile and expense list.
* @param expenseList the user's current list of expenses.
* Computes the percentage of the BTO goal reached.
* Returns {@code 0} if the BTO goal is zero or negative.
* @return percentage of goal reached, rounded to the nearest whole number.
* Computes a human-readable estimate of when the BTO goal will be reached.
* @return {@code "Reached! Go get that BTO!"} if the goal is met,
*         {@code "Infinite (Surplus is $0 or negative!)"} if no progress can be made,
*         or the estimated number of months as a {@code String} otherwise.
* Handles all user-facing input and output operations for FinTrackPro.

*

This class centralizes console interaction logic, ensuring that:

*
  • All output formatting passes through a single interface

*
  • Business logic remains separated from presentation logic

*

This class does not perform validation or computation;
* it is strictly responsible for communication with the user.
* Displays the welcome message and ASCII logo when the application starts.

*

This method is intended to be called once at application startup.
Secure the Keys, Secure the Dream
printLine("I am FinBro's brother, FinTrack Pro!");
printLine("Goodbye " + name + ". Stay disciplined and get that house that you always wanted!");
* Displays an optional prompt and reads a full line of input from the user.

*

If the prompt is null or empty, no prompt is displayed.
* @param in Scanner used to read user input.
* @param prompt Prompt message displayed before reading input.
* @return The raw line entered by the user.
public String readLine(Scanner in, String prompt){
* Displays a list of supported commands and their usage.

*

This method prints the currently supported CLI commands
* and brief descriptions of their purpose.
printLine("'summary' - generate your BTO readiness report based on your goals");
printLine("'add'      <amount> - add a new expense (e.g., add 5.50)");
printLine("'list'     - view all current expenses and your total spent");
printLine("'delete'   <index> - remove a specific expense from your list");
printLine("'savings' - add a surplus amount to your existing savings");
printLine("'clear'   - wipe all current expenses from the list");
printLine("'reset'   - wipes all profile data and expenses to start fresh.");
* Prints a formatted BTO Readiness Report to the console.
* @param report a {@link SummaryReport} containing the user's precomputed financial snapshot.
* Utility class for validated user input handling in FinTrackPro.

*

This class performs input validation loops and only returns values
* once valid input has been received.
* Formats a monetary amount using US currency formatting.
* Prompts the user to enter a monetary value and validates the input.

*

This method repeatedly prompts until valid input is received.

*

Note: The returned value represents 2.5% of the entered amount
* @param ui UI component used for displaying prompts and messages.
* @param in Scanner used to read user input.
* @param prompt Prompt message shown to the user.
public static BigDecimal readMoney(Ui ui, Scanner in, String prompt) {
ui.printLine("Bruh I need a valid amount like " +
"10250 or 10250.50 (numbers only, max 2 dp). Try again.");
ui.printLine("The amount cannot be negative. Please try again.");
* Prompts the user to enter a future date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).

*
  • Date must be strictly after today's date

*

If validation fails, the user is re-prompted until valid input is provided.
* @param ui UI component used for displaying prompts and messages.
* @param in Scanner used to read user input.
* @param prompt Prompt message shown to the user.
* @return A {@link LocalDate} that is strictly after today.
public static LocalDate readFutureDate(Ui ui, Scanner in, String prompt) {
ui.printLine("Deadline must be a future date. Try again.");
ui.printLine("Date format needs to be YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2026-12-31). Try again.");
* Prompts the user to enter a contribution ratio and validates the input range.

*

This method repeatedly prompts until a valid ratio is received.
* @param ui UI component used for displaying prompts and messages.
* @param in Scanner used to read user input.
* @param prompt Prompt message shown to the user.
public static BigDecimal readRatio(Ui ui, Scanner in, String prompt) {
// Regex: Digits followed by a dot and 1-2 digits.
// This prevents negative signs (-), symbols, and excessive decimals.
ui.printLine("EH WRONG FORMAT! Enter a decimal between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.5).");
ui.printLine("Brother...Ratio must be between 0 and 1. Try again!");
ui.printLine("Pleaseee input a number. Try again!!!");
